propositions: Operation certificates are made asymmetric, signed by an environment's Ed25519 key and verifiable against its public key so tickets/state cannot be replayed.
A new opcert.py module registers rebar.opcert.v1 pinned to the sshsig scheme in the registry POLICY table and is wired into attest/__init__.py.

The certificate subject is an in-toto v1 Statement binding ticket id, material fingerprint, merged-log main commit position, and kind, using the existing canonical gate fingerprint treated as an opaque string.
A cert must not be replayable onto a different ticket or a mutated material fingerprint, since the verifier compares the cert's bound fingerprint against the ticket's current one.

Environment-key rotation uses an explicit-SHA era model: a key is valid at commit P iff is-ancestor(added_at_commit,P) and not is-ancestor(revoked_at_commit,P), reusing the shared ancestry rule rather than resolve_event_commit.
The op-cert verifier performs a two-phase check emitting canonical verdicts certified, key_not_valid_at_era, or mismatch without introducing a new verdict string.
